steps.title: Use slope-intercept form
steps.content:
A linear function with slope $3$ can be written as follows, where $b$ is the y-intercept.
$$g(x)=3x+b$$

steps.title: Substitute the given point
steps.content:
The graph passes through $(2,19)$, so plug in $x=2$ and $g(x)=19$.
$$19=3(2)+b$$
$$19=6+b$$
$$13=b$$

steps.title: Write the function
steps.content:
Substitute $b=13$ into the linear equation.
$$g(x)=3x+13$$
The answer is C.
